channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: LASTMA arrests tanker driver for killing dispatch rider in Lagos
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
LASTMA has arrested a tanker driver who fatally struck a dispatch rider in Lagos. The driver attempted to flee but was apprehended by vigilant officers.
Read More: https://punchng.com/lastma-arrests-tanker-driver-for-killing-dispatch-rider-in-lagos/

item.title: MLS probes Inter Miami over Casemiro transfer
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
MLS has launched an investigation into Inter Miami regarding the transfer of Brazilian midfielder Casemiro, focusing on discovery rights. Find out more.
Read More: https://punchng.com/2196862-2/

item.title: Kano declares readiness for state police
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kano State Governor, Abba Yusuf, declares the state is ready for state police, citing institutional capacity and political will to enhance security.
Read More: https://punchng.com/kano-declares-readiness-for-state-police/

item.title: NPC begins digital birth, death registration in Kwara
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The National Population Commission (NPC) has launched digital birth and death registration in Kwara State, enhancing legal identity and demographic data.
Read More: https://punchng.com/npc-begins-digital-birth-death-registration-in-kwara/

item.title: Bayelsa denies plan to relocate NDU College of Health Sciences
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Bayelsa State Government denies reports of relocating Niger Delta University (NDU) College of Health Sciences, calling the speculation false and unfounded.
Read More: https://punchng.com/bayelsa-denies-plan-to-relocate-ndu-college-of-health-sciences/

item.title: Nigeria’s digital identity transformation unrivaled under Tinubu — Shettima
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
VP Shettima hails Nigeria’s unrivaled digital ID transformation under Tinubu, driven by NIMC. Learn how the NIMC Act 2026 is building a sovereign digital p
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigerias-digital-identity-transformation-unrivaled-under-tinubu-shettima/

item.title: Aston Villa agree deal to sign Garnacho
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Aston Villa have agreed a deal to sign Alejandro Garnacho from Chelsea.

item.title: Bayelsa deputy gov urges women to lead vanguard for social change
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Bayelsa Deputy Governor Peter Akpe calls on women to lead social change, emphasizing their critical role in nation-building and addressing societal challen
Read More: https://punchng.com/bayelsa-deputy-gov-urges-women-to-lead-vanguard-for-social-change/

item.title: Mali, Niger, Burkina Faso bid to host AFCON 2032
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Mali, Niger, and Burkina Faso are jointly bidding to host the 2032 Africa Cup of Nations. Learn more about their historic AFCON 2032 bid.
Read More: https://punchng.com/mali-niger-burkina-faso-bid-to-host-afcon-2032/

item.title: Intimidation wont be allowed during Osun poll, says Oyebamiji
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
APC candidate Bola Oyebamiji assures Osun voters that intimidation will not be tolerated during the August 15 Osun election. Collect your PVC and vote with
Read More: https://punchng.com/intimidation-wont-be-allowed-during-osun-poll-says-oyebamiji/

item.title: Wolves cancel training session after Arokodare transfer protest
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Tolu Arokodare’s transfer protest led Wolves to cancel training as the striker pushes for a move to Fiorentina, rejecting Trabzonspor’s offer.
Read More: https://punchng.com/wolves-cancel-training-session-after-arokodare-transfer-protest/

item.title: Exclusive: In Syria's Kurdish-majority city Kobane, postwar integration efforts get under way
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Northern Syria’s Kobane, or Ayn al-Arab, was a flashpoint city during the Kurdish fight against the Islamic State (IS) group nearly a decade ago. Today, it’s at the heart of the Syrian government’s bid to integrate the northern Kurdish majority region and their fighters. FRANCE 24’s Wassim Nasr had

item.title: Tinubu’s digital identity reforms ‘unrivalled’, says Shettima
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Vice-President Kashim Shettima on Wednesday praised the President Bola Tinubu administration's overhaul of Nigeria's digital identity system, describing the ongoing reforms led by the National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) as "unrivalled". The post Tinubu’s digital identity reforms ‘unrivall

item.title: Fubara endorses Kingsley Chinda as successor, declares peace with Wike
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Governor of Rivers State, Siminalayi Fubara has endorsed the governorship candidate of All Progressives Congress (APC), Kingsley Chinda as his preferred successor while declaring peace with his predecessor, Nyesom Wike. The post Fubara endorses Kingsley Chinda as successor, declares peace with Wike

item.title: Senate Suspends Consideration Of Coast Guard Bill, Seeks More Consultations
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Senate has stepped down further consideration of the Nigeria Coast Guard (Establishment) Bill, 2026 (SB. 575), to allow for additional consultations among stakeholders. The decision followed the presentation of the report of the Senate Committee on Marine Transport on the bill during Wednesday’s

item.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Karen Carter was found with stab wounds in the Dordogne village she had lived in for over a decade.

item.title: Here’s how to use salary-transparency laws to get paid more
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Virginia and Maine are the latest states pushing employers to disclose salary in their job listings.

item.title: Dangote Refinery resumes petrol loading in naira at N1,215/litre
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: …As depots, filling stations adjust prices By Udeme Akpan, Energy Editor Dangote Petroleum Refinery has resumed gantry loading of Premium Motor Spirit (PMS), also known as petrol, in naira after a week-long suspension, ending uncertainty in the downstream sector over its temporary shift to dol

item.title: N152m Disputed Property: Court Reserves Ruling On Admissibility Of Documents Till October 27
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Justice Musa Kakaki of Federal High Court, sitting Ikoyi, has fixed ruling for October 27, on admissibility of documents sought to be tendered by the prosecution in the ongoing trial of property developer Olukayode Olusanya and his company, Oak Homes Ltd. over an alleged N152 million property disput

item.title: Police top list as ICPC exposes 908 ghost workers across MDAs
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission, ICPC, has revealed that it uncovered 908 ghost workers across several federal ministries, departments and agencies, MDAs, in a payroll fraud scheme that allegedly drained hundreds of millions of naira from government coffers. T

item.title: FMBN to complete 962 Abuja homes after recovery of $65 million housing project
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Federal Mortgage Bank of Nigeria (FMBN) is set to complete 962 housing units in Abuja after the recovery of a housing project financed with a $65 million loan that was allegedly diverted. The post FMBN to complete 962 Abuja homes after recovery of $65 million housing project appeared first on Na

item.title: Senate Moves To Sanction NNPCL, CBN, TETFUND, FAAN, Others Over Revenue Remittances
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Senate has threatened to invoke its constitutional powers against Ministries, Departments and Agencies (MDAs) that have refused to honour invitations by the Senate Committee on Finance for investigative hearings. The warning followed a point of order raised by the Chairman of the Committee on Fi

item.title: Villa agree loan deal for Chelsea winger Garnacho
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Aston Villa agree a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until the end of the 2026-27 season, with an obligation to buy.

item.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Donald Trump says the US will respond any time Iranian forces shoot at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Super Eagles: ‘Nobody told me why’ – Seyi Olofinjana laments being left out of World Cup squad
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Super Eagles midfielder, Seyi Olofinjana, has shared his thoughts on being left out of Nigeria’s squad for the 2010 FIFA World Cup, saying that no one even cared to explain his omission despite his significant contributions during the qualification process. Olofinjana, who represented t

item.title: The chip sector’s recent calm is masking a turbulent outlook in the options market
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Relatively high volatility readings for chips warn there could be further downside — and that it’s not yet worth the risk to buy the dip.

item.title: Kano ready for state police – Gov Yusuf
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Kano State Governor, Abba Kabir Yusuf, says the state is ready for the creation of a state police system, describing it as an important step towards improving security and protecting lives and property. The governor made the statement on Wednesday while inaugurating the Inter-Agencies Task Force on

item.title: Oyo Abductions: Stakeholders reject ethnic profiling, seek unity against insecurity
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Adeola Badru Stakeholders in Oyo State have called for stronger collaboration among security agencies, traditional institutions, farmers, herders and local communities to tackle kidnapping and other security challenges across Nigeria. They also warned against the ethnic profiling of Fulani people

item.title: 2027: Five South-East Governors Resolve To Back Tinubu’s Re-Election, Says Uzodimma
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The governor said the South-East could no longer be swayed by political rhetoric when evidence of federal intervention across the zone was visible. The post 2027: Five South-East Governors Resolve To Back Tinubu’s Re-Election, Says Uzodimma appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Service-Wide Vote: APC slams Atiku, says ex-VP ‘more disoriented and misguided than ever before’
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: APC says, as a former vice president and a long-standing presidential aspirant, Atiku ought to understand the meaning and purpose of a Service-Wide Vote. The post Service-Wide Vote: APC slams Atiku, says ex-VP ‘more disoriented and misguided than ever before’ appeared first on Premium Ti

item.title: Osun Poly Denies Running Illegal Summer School Programme
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The management of Osun State Polytechnic (OSPOLY), Iree, has dismissed allegations circulating on social media that it operates an illegal summer school programme through which Higher National Diploma (HND) certificates are awarded after only a few days of lectures and examinations. The institution

item.title: ‘We will not give automatic presidential ticket to any aspirant’ — NNPP
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Founder and Chairman of the Board of Trustees of the New Nigeria Peoples Party (NNPP), Dr Boniface Aniebonam, has said the party will not give an automatic presidential ticket to any aspirant ahead of the 2027 general elections. Aniebonam, in a statement issued on Wednesday in Lagos, said every

item.title: El-Rufai Absent As Court Adjourns Ruling On Recusal Application To September 24
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Kaduna State governor, Malam Nasir El-Rufai, was absent in court on Wednesday as a Kaduna State High Court adjourned until September 24, 2026, for ruling on his application seeking the recusal of the presiding judge from his ongoing trial. The presiding judge, Justice Darius Khobo, fixed the

item.title: Court stops EFCC from investigating Oyo finances
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A Federal High Court sitting in Ibadan, the Oyo State capital, has restrained the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) from investigating the finances of the Oyo State Government. The court stopped the planned investigation on Wednesday. The court, while halting the process, described the

item.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

item.title: Financial Services Sector Seen as Focus for Activist Campaigns
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Financial services companies are attracting activist investors who see them as generating cash that could be paid out to investors, said Paul Weiss’ Carmen Lu.

item.title: REA: Fake federalist – Atiku’s aide hits Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Demola Olarenwaju, the Senior Political Assistant to the African Democratic Congress, NDC, 2027 presidential candidate, Atiku Abubakar, has described President Bola Tinubu as a fake federalist. Olarenwaju made the remark while noting the federal government as no business keeping the Rural Electrific

item.description: The Media Office of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) presidential candidate, Atiku Abubakar, has accused the All Progressives Congress (APC) of failing to address concerns over the ₦12.8 trillion Service-Wide Vote (SWV) contained in the 2026 Appropriation Act, insisting that the ruling party’s

item.title: Kano Hisbah warns residents against paying for mass wedding registration
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Kano State Hisbah Board has warned residents not to pay anyone to be registered for the state’s upcoming mass wedding programme. The board’s Deputy Commander-General, Dr Mujahideen Aminuddeen, gave the warning on Wednesday following reports that some individuals were collecting money from people

item.title: Unlocking Value in Nigeria’s Capital Markets: Opportunities Across Fixed Income and Equities
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Nigeria’s capital market is undergoing one of the most significant transformations in its modern history. Economic reforms, evolving monetary policy, increasing domestic participation and the growing sophistication of institutional investors are reshaping the investment landscape. While market

item.title: Mali, Niger and Burkina Faso bid to host AFCON 2032
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Mali, Niger and Burkina Faso -- three countries led by military governments -- are bidding to jointly host the 2032 Africa Cup of Nations. The post Mali, Niger and Burkina Faso bid to host AFCON 2032 appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: FIFA has officially announced the fan-voted Best XI of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, celebrating the players who captivated supporters with their outstanding performances throughout the tournament. The World Champions Spain lead the way with three players in the lineup. Runners-up Argentina have two repr

item.title: Fashola Hails Tinubu On Economic Reforms

item.description: Former Lagos State Governor and Minister of Works and Housing, Babatunde Raji FasholaBabatunde Raji Fashola (SAN), has commended President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s economic reform agenda, describing the policies as difficult but necessary for Nigeria’s long-term growth and stability. item.description: The newly appointed leader says Hamas would work to help the people of Gaza and achieve a united Palestinian state.

item.title: Reps Okay Bill For Rural Infrastructure Development Commission
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The House of Representatives on Wednesday passed for second reading a bill seeking to establish the National Commission for Rural Infrastructure and Development (NACRID), a proposed institution designed to provide a coordinated framework for financing, monitoring and supporting rural infrastructure

item.title: Death toll from blast at Indian hydroelectric project rises to 20 people
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Rescuers say flooding and toxic gas in the collapsed tunnel make it unlikely any remaining trapped workers survived.

item.description: NAFDAC described the banned sachet and sub-200ml PET bottled alcoholic drinks as illegal products that should no longer be in circulation. The post NAFDAC begins nationwide seizure of sachet alcohol appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.description: For more than a decade, Big Brother Naija has remained one of Africa’s biggest television events. item.title: Venezuela Oil: Batistas’ Fluxus Clinches Stake in PDVSA Joint Venture
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Brazil’s billionaire Batista brothers have acquired a stake in an oil production project in Venezuela, the latest deal to emerge in a country that the Trump administration is vowing to reinvigorate after years of sanctions.

item.title: Olapegba emerges University of Ibadan’s 14th vice chancellor
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Governing Council of the University of Ibadan (UI) has appointed Professor Peter Olamakinde Olapegba as the 14th substantive Vice-Chancellor of Nigeria's premier university, following a rigorous and competitive selection process. The post Olapegba emerges University of Ibadan’s 14th vice c

item.title: Joy in Adeleke family as governor’s daughter bags Mass Communication degree
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Omolola recently completed her studies and earned a degree in Mass Communication, a milestone that her father described as a remarkable achievement.

item.title: Governor Yusuf Declares Kano Ready For State Police
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The governor said the proposed state police would work in partnership with the Nigeria Police Force. The post Governor Yusuf Declares Kano Ready For State Police appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: High Cost Of Food Puts Healthy Diet Beyond Reach Of 191m Nigerians – UN
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: About 191.2 million Nigerians were unable to afford a healthy diet in 2025 as rising food costs continued to push adequate nutrition beyond the reach of millions, according to a new United Nations (UN) report. The 2026 State of Food Security and Nutrition in the World report revealed that the number

item.title: Americans are skipping lettuce and salads as investigation into cyclospora outbreak continues
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Shares of restaurant chains like Cava, Chipotle and Sweetgreen have come under pressure.

item.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Major League Soccer says it is investigating allegations of tampering by Inter Miami after the club signed Brazil midfielder Casemiro.

item.description: Barring the minute changes, the House of Representatives' Ad-hoc Committee investigating controversies surrounding the establishment... The post PFIPC probe: Reps to quiz NSA, IGP, Oyedele, others Thursday appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Appeal Court affirms final forfeiture of N293.9m, properties linked to retired general
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Emmanuel Agbo The post Appeal Court affirms final forfeiture of N293.9m, properties linked to retired general appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Court stops EFCC’s planned probe of Oyo finances, declares request speculative
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A Federal High Court sitting in Ibadan has restrained the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) from proceeding with its planned... The post Court stops EFCC’s planned probe of Oyo finances, declares request speculative appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy explains why Anthropic is winning (and it’s not the model)
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy says he’s never seen in 25 years of investing, not in the interne

item.title: Senate moves to withhold NDLEA, NRC, DSS, other MDA budgets for flouting resolutions
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Federal government Ministries, Departments, and Agencies (MDAs) that fail to implement or respond to resolutions of the Senate risk the withholding of their budgetary allocations following a resolution passed by lawmakers on Wednesday. The post Senate moves to withhold NDLEA, NRC, DSS, other MDA bud

item.title: Agbonlahor tags Aston Villa’s pursuit of Garnacho ‘nightmare’
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Aston Villa legend, Gabriel Agbonlahor has branded the club’s reported pursuit of Alejandro Garnacho a “nightmare”, insisting the Chelsea winger is not good enough for Villa Park. The post Agbonlahor tags Aston Villa’s pursuit of Garnacho ‘nightmare’ appeared first on The Sun Niger

item.title: US judge sets 2027 trial date for Venezuela’s Maduro and wife
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Former President Maduro pleads not guilty to drug charges; lawyer plans to challenge legality of US military operation.

item.title: 5 kitchen ingredients that keep wall lizards out of your home
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Wall lizards are a common nuisance in many households. While they are technically harmless and help control the insect population, their droppings, unsightly presence, and the startle factor they bring are rarely welcome. item.title: ‘I have a $2,000 deductible’: I reported a cracked windshield on my Hertz rental car. Can they charge me?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I never heard a rock hit the windshield.”

item.title: 24,000 treated for suspected cholera in Borno
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: No fewer than 24,000 people have been treated for suspected cholera in Borno State, north-eastern Nigeria, as thousands of people.... The post 24,000 treated for suspected cholera in Borno appeared first on Tribune Online.
